sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, ts1 TIMESTAMP, ts2 TIMESTAMP, value NUMERIC);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '1992-09-21 20:38:48', TIMESTAMP '1992-03-22 01:02:03.1234', 10.5);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (2, TIMESTAMP '2000-01-01 00:00:00', TIMESTAMP '2000-01-01 00:00:01', 20.7);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (3, TIMESTAMP '1980-05-15 12:00:00', TIMESTAMP NULL, 5.0);
SELECT id, greatest(ts1, ts2) * value AS computed_value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (greatest(ts1, ts2) * 2.0) AS scaled_value FROM test_table WHERE greatest(ts1, ts2) IS NOT NULL ORDER BY scaled_value DESC;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY greatest(ts1, ts2)) AS count_per_timestamp FROM test_table;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE greatest(ts1, ts2) * (CASE WHEN value IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0;
SELECT id, greatest(ts1, ts2) * (SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_table WHERE value > 0) AS average_scaled_value FROM test_table;
WITH temp AS (SELECT id, greatest(ts1, ts2) AS max_ts FROM test_table WHERE ts1 IS NOT NULL) SELECT id, max_ts * 1.5 FROM temp;
